title: senecio segethi
intro: Senecio segethi, also known as groundsel or cornfield knapweed, is a plant species in the daisy family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Senecio segethi include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be cautioned due to potential toxicity and the risk of liver damage.
